text: Time MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that provides time and timezone conversion capabilities. This server enables LLMs to get current time information and perform timezone conversions using IANA timezone names, with automatic system timezone detection. Available Tools: get_current_time - Get current time in a specific timezone or system timezone. Required arguments: timezone (string): IANA timezone name (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'). convert_time - Convert time between timezones. Required arguments: source_timezone, time (HH:MM), target_timezone.
source: verbatim (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/time/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: mcp-server-sentry: A Sentry MCP server. Overview: A Model Context Protocol server for retrieving and analyzing issues from Sentry.io. This server provides tools to inspect error reports, stacktraces, and other debugging information from your Sentry account. Tools: get_sentry_issue - Retrieve and analyze a Sentry issue by ID or URL. Input: issue_id_or_url (string): Sentry issue ID or URL to analyze. Returns: Issue details including Title, Issue ID, Status, Level, First seen timestamp, Last seen timestamp, Event count, Full stacktrace.
source: verbatim (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/sentry/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: PostgreSQL. A Model Context Protocol server that provides read-only access to PostgreSQL databases. This server enables LLMs to inspect database schemas and execute read-only queries. Components: Tools: query - Execute read-only SQL queries against the connected database. Input: sql (string): The SQL query to execute. All queries are executed within a READ ONLY transaction. Resources: The server provides schema information for each table in the database: Table Schemas (postgres://<host>/<table>/schema). Column names and data types are returned as JSON, allowing inspection of the database structure.
source: verbatim (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/postgres/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: Knowledge Graph Memory Server. A basic implementation of persistent memory using a local knowledge graph. This lets Claude remember information about the user across chats. Core Concepts: Entities. Entities are the primary nodes in the knowledge graph. Each entity has a unique name (identifier), an entity type (e.g., 'person', 'organization', 'event'), and a list of observations. Relations connect entities directionally and describe how one entity relates to another. Observations are atomic facts attached to a single entity.
source: verbatim+authored-extension (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/memory/README.md; observations sentence is authored representative)

text: Sequential Thinking MCP Server. An MCP server implementation that provides a tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through a structured thinking process. Features: Break down complex problems into manageable steps; Revise and refine thoughts as understanding deepens; Branch into alternative paths of reasoning; Adjust the total number of thoughts dynamically; Generate and verify solution hypotheses. The single tool, sequentialthinking, accepts a thought string, a thought number, a total thought count, and a flag indicating whether another thought is required.
source: verbatim+authored-extension (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/sequentialthinking/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19; tool-signature sentence authored from public schema)

text: Everything MCP Server. This MCP server attempts to exercise all the features of the MCP protocol. It is not intended to be a useful server, but rather a test server for builders of MCP clients. It implements prompts, tools, resources, sampling, and more to showcase MCP capabilities. Usage with Claude Desktop (uses stdio Transport): Add to your claude_desktop_config.json a mcpServers entry pointing at npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything. The server is useful for verifying that a client correctly handles each MCP primitive.
source: verbatim+authored-extension (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/everything/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: Filesystem MCP Server. A Node.js implementation that enables MCP clients to interact with the file system through a standardized protocol. Core capabilities include reading and writing files, creating and managing directories, relocating files and folders, and searching across the file system. Read-only operations such as read_text_file, read_media_file, and read_multiple_files retrieve file contents. Directory navigation is handled through list_directory, list_directory_with_sizes, and directory_tree. Access is restricted to directories configured at startup via command-line arguments or MCP Roots.
source: paraphrased (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/filesystem/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19 — WebFetch returned model-summarised text)

text: mcp-server-git. A Model Context Protocol server for Git repository interaction and automation. This tool enables large language models to read, search, and manipulate Git repositories through a collection of specialised functions. The server offers twelve primary capabilities: git_status, git_diff_unstaged, git_diff_staged, and git_diff for examining repository changes; git_add, git_reset, and git_commit for managing changes; git_log supports filtering by date ranges; git_create_branch, git_checkout, and git_branch for branch work; git_show for inspecting commit contents.
source: paraphrased (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/git/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19 — WebFetch returned model-summarised text)

text: Fetch MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that provides web content fetching capabilities. It allows language models to retrieve and process web page content by converting HTML to markdown format. The server includes a primary fetch tool with parameters for URL specification, maximum content length (default 5000 characters), starting position for chunked reading, and an option for raw content retrieval. A security caution is prominently featured, warning that this server can access local/internal IP addresses and may represent a security risk.
source: paraphrased (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers src/fetch/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: GitHub MCP Server. The GitHub MCP server provides tools that enable language models to interact with GitHub repositories. Supported tools include create_or_update_file, search_repositories, create_issue, list_commits, fork_repository, create_pull_request, and list_pull_request_files. The server reads a GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN from the environment for authentication. All write operations are gated behind the token's scope; if the token is read-only, write tools will return permission errors from the GitHub API rather than silently failing.
source: authored representative (could not WebFetch full README cleanly; tool list cross-referenced with github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/github/README.md, fetched 2026-05-19)

text: GitLab MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that exposes a subset of the GitLab REST API. Tools include create_or_update_file, push_files, search_repositories, create_repository, get_file_contents, create_issue, create_merge_request, fork_repository, and create_branch. The server expects GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN and GITLAB_API_URL (defaults to https://gitlab.com/api/v4) in the environment. All requests are made with that token; the server itself enforces no additional authorisation.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/gitlab/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: Google Drive MCP Server. This server provides access to Google Drive files and folders via the Model Context Protocol. It supports listing files (search), reading file contents (read), and resolving Google-native formats (Docs, Sheets, Slides) to their exported equivalents. Authentication uses OAuth 2.0; on first run the server opens a browser to obtain a refresh token and writes it to a credentials file. Subsequent runs use the stored refresh token to mint short-lived access tokens.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/gdrive/README.md publicly documented behaviour)

text: Slack MCP Server. Provides tools for reading and posting to Slack workspaces. Tools include slack_list_channels, slack_post_message, slack_reply_to_thread, slack_add_reaction, slack_get_channel_history, slack_get_thread_replies, slack_get_users, and slack_get_user_profile. The server uses a SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (xoxb-...) and SLACK_TEAM_ID. Required scopes vary by tool; reading channel history requires channels:history, posting requires chat:write, and reactions require reactions:write.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/slack/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: Brave Search MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that wraps the Brave Search API. Two tools are exposed: brave_web_search for general queries and brave_local_search for finding nearby businesses and points of interest. Both accept a query string and an optional count (1-20). The server reads BRAVE_API_KEY from the environment. Rate limits follow the Brave Search plan associated with the key; 429 responses are surfaced to the caller as tool errors.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/brave-search/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: Puppeteer MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server providing browser automation via Puppeteer. Tools: puppeteer_navigate (url), puppeteer_screenshot (name, selector, width, height), puppeteer_click (selector), puppeteer_fill (selector, value), puppeteer_select (selector, value), puppeteer_hover (selector), and puppeteer_evaluate (script). Useful for scraping rendered pages, automating form submissions, and capturing visual regression evidence. The server runs a headless Chromium subprocess; the host machine must have the required libraries.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/puppeteer/README.md publicly documented tool list)

text: EverArt MCP Server. Exposes the EverArt image generation API as MCP tools. Primary tool: generate_image (prompt, model, image_count). Supported models include flux1, sd3, sd3.5, recraft-real, recraft-vector. The server reads EVERART_API_KEY from the environment. Generated images are returned as URLs hosted by EverArt; the calling agent is responsible for downloading or embedding them as needed.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/everart/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: AWS Knowledge Base Retrieval MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that queries Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases via the RetrieveAndGenerate API. The retrieve tool takes a knowledgeBaseId, a query string, and an optional numberOfResults (default 5). Authentication uses standard AWS credentials resolved through the SDK chain: environment variables, shared credentials file, or IAM role. Cross-region knowledge bases require AWS_REGION to be set explicitly.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/aws-kb-retrieval-server/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: Google Maps MCP Server. Provides geocoding, place lookup, directions, and elevation queries against the Google Maps Platform APIs. Tools: maps_geocode (address), maps_reverse_geocode (latitude, longitude), maps_search_places (query, location, radius), maps_place_details (place_id), maps_distance_matrix (origins[], destinations[], mode), maps_elevation (locations[]), maps_directions (origin, destination, mode). Requires GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY with the appropriate APIs enabled in the GCP console.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/google-maps/README.md publicly documented tool list)

text: Redis MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server that exposes Redis as a tool surface. Tools: set (key, value, expireSeconds?), get (key), delete (key), list (pattern). Connection is configured via a single redis:// URL (REDIS_URL env var or first positional argument). The server uses node-redis under the hood and supports both single-node and clustered deployments via the standard URL format.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/redis/README.md publicly documented surface)

text: SQLite MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server providing read/write access to a local SQLite database file. Tools: read_query (sql), write_query (sql), create_table (sql), list_tables, describe_table (table_name), append_insight (insight). The server is configured with a database path passed as a CLI argument. All queries run against that single file; there is no schema isolation or per-tool access control beyond what the user grants the calling agent.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived src/sqlite/README.md publicly documented tool list; fetch returned 404)

text: Cloudflare MCP Server. A Model Context Protocol server for managing Cloudflare resources via the Cloudflare API. Provides tools for KV namespace operations (kv_get, kv_put, kv_delete, kv_list_keys), R2 bucket operations, Workers deployment, and zone/DNS management. Uses CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN with appropriate scopes. The server is published under the cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare repository and is built on top of the official cloudflare/cloudflare-typescript SDK.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare publicly documented surface)

text: Stripe Agent Toolkit (MCP). The Stripe Agent Toolkit provides an MCP server wrapping the Stripe API. Tools include create_customer, list_customers, create_product, create_price, create_payment_link, create_invoice, list_invoices, create_refund, retrieve_balance. Authentication uses a Stripe restricted key; the toolkit will reject requests if the key lacks the required scopes for a given tool. Designed for use with restricted keys scoped tightly to the operations an agent should be permitted to perform.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from github.com/stripe/agent-toolkit publicly documented surface)

text: Notion MCP Server. A community-maintained MCP server that wraps the Notion API. Tools include search (query, filter), retrieve_page (page_id), retrieve_database (database_id), query_database (database_id, filter, sorts), create_page (parent, properties, children), update_page (page_id, properties), append_block_children (block_id, children). Authentication uses a Notion internal integration token; the integration must be added to each page or database it needs to access.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from community Notion MCP server READMEs; surface mirrors official Notion API)

text: Linear MCP Server. Provides MCP tools for interacting with Linear, the issue tracker. Tools: linear_list_issues (filter), linear_get_issue (id), linear_create_issue (team_id, title, description, priority, labels), linear_update_issue (id, status, assignee), linear_add_comment (issue_id, body), linear_list_teams, linear_list_projects. Requires LINEAR_API_KEY (personal API key from the Linear settings page). The server uses Linear's GraphQL API under the hood.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from community Linear MCP server READMEs; mirrors Linear GraphQL public surface)

text: Kubernetes MCP Server. Exposes a subset of kubectl functionality as MCP tools. Tools: list_pods (namespace), get_pod (namespace, name), pod_logs (namespace, name, container, tail_lines), list_deployments (namespace), describe_resource (kind, name, namespace), apply_manifest (yaml), delete_resource (kind, name, namespace). The server uses the kubeconfig at $KUBECONFIG (default ~/.kube/config) and acts with whatever RBAC permissions that context grants. Destructive tools can be disabled via the --read-only flag.
source: authored representative (paraphrased from multiple community Kubernetes MCP server READMEs; mirrors kubectl public surface)
